notes: Official Maine wastewater resources page stating that homeowners should visit the town office for the HHE-200 form first, that an online septic permit search may also help, and that site evaluation and installation permits are often paired

agency_name: Maine Center for Disease Control and Prevention
title: Frequently asked questions about septic systems and wells
notes: Official Maine FAQ PDF stating that the fastest option is to visit the town office for the HHE-200 design and permit application and that statewide database availability is not guaranteed for all septic designs

agency_name: Maine Center for Disease Control and Prevention
title: HHE 200-204 Updates
notes: Official Maine HHE update page stating that since July 1 1974 a town office should keep copies of approved HHE-200 forms, that the state is digitizing those permits, and that the issuing town office relies on the installer portion during Local Plumbing Inspector inspections

agency_name: Maine Center for Disease Control and Prevention
title: Subsurface Wastewater Licensing and Certification
notes: Official Maine licensing page stating that a Local Plumbing Inspector performs inspections of licensed work and that HHE-200 forms are the system designs for which permits are issued and inspections are performed

agency_name: Maine Center for Disease Control and Prevention
title: How Do I Find My Permit?
notes: Official Maine permit-search guide explaining how to search the septic plans database by town address and HHE application type

agency_name: New Hampshire Department of Environmental Services
title: SSB-14 Transfers of Waterfront Properties with Subsurface Systems
notes: Official NHDES fact sheet stating that from 9/1/2024 certain developed protected-shoreland properties with ISDS within 250 feet require septic evaluation and, for older or unapproved systems, designer review

title: NHDES - Septic (Subsurface)
notes: Official New Hampshire online forms portal for septic subsurface workflow and NHDES septic filings

agency_name: New Hampshire Department of Environmental Services
title: The Municipal EcoLink April 2023
notes: Official NHDES bulletin stating that property owners must have proper septic system permits to utilize properties as intended, proof of an operationally approved septic system may be needed for existing or proposed use, and some expansions require Individual Sewage Disposal System application and approval

agency_name: New Hampshire Department of Environmental Services
title: The Municipal EcoLink March 2024
notes: Official NHDES bulletin stating that septic system records access is getting easier and faster and that homeowners should check OneStop first

agency_name: New Hampshire Department of Environmental Services
title: SSB-1 Replacement of a Failed Subsurface Disposal System
notes: Official NHDES fact sheet stating that the town health officer permitted designer or other local official must verify failure in writing for replacement applications and that construction approval is tied to a 90-day completion window

agency_name: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management
title: Septic & Onsite Wastewater Treatment Systems
notes: Official Rhode Island OWTS hub stating DEM has records of most systems from 1968 forward, that historic and active permit searches are available online, that full-file requests can be made by record request, and that some areas require more advanced technology

agency_name: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management
title: OWR Online Permit Searches
notes: Official Rhode Island permit-search page stating that the public can use the active permit search to view application status history materials and approved plans and that historic OWTS permit searches remain available

agency_name: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management
title: Onsite Wastewater Treatment Disposal
notes: Official Rhode Island compliance page linking the Septic System Checkup handbook and noting that complaints and failure issues are part of the public compliance record

agency_name: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management
title: Residential OWTS Suitability Determination Fact Sheet
notes: Official Rhode Island fact sheet stating that a suitability determination helps owners know if a renovation will require an OWTS permit and that suitability depends on the size and status of the current system

agency_name: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management
title: OWR Permit Application Portal
notes: Official Rhode Island portal page stating that the public may use the Active Permit Search to view application status history application materials and final permit documents including approved plans and permits

title: Wastewater System and Potable Water Supply Program
notes: Official Vermont wastewater program page stating that the program issues wastewater and potable-water permits through five regional offices and pointing landowners to regional office contacts and permitting information

title: Permit Search Guide
notes: Official Vermont permit-search guide explaining how to search for state-issued wastewater and potable-water permits by address permit number or town and how to determine if a state-issued permit exists

title: Wastewater System & Potable Water Supply Permit Application FAQ
notes: Official Vermont FAQ stating that applicants should check with the Town the lot is located in and use permit resources to determine whether a wastewater and potable water permit is required

title: Wastewater System and Potable Water Supply Rules Effective 11/06/2023
notes: Official Vermont rules PDF stating that a permit is required for construction or modification and that applications are submitted to the appropriate Agency Regional Office

title: Shoreland Permitting
notes: Official Vermont shoreland page stating that wastewater systems permitted by the Agency are exempt from separate shoreland permits in some cases and that delegated municipalities may handle shoreland permitting locally
